LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER CHARLOTTE DANIELS WRIT OF MANDATE. RULING: DISCUSSED AND PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR ANY ADDITIONAL TIME WAS DENIED BY THE COURT, NO GOOD CAUSE TO CONTINUE THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXHAUST HER ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES, FAILS TO CITE TO THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD OR APPLICABLE LAW. THE PROJECT HAS MET ITS CEQA OBLIGATIONS, IS NOT SUBJECT TO THE NATIONAL HISTORIC PRESERVATION ACT AND THE COURT HAS FOUND THAT SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE SUPPORTS DEFENDANT'S FINDINGS. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. ON THE RECORD, COURT ORDERED THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD BE RETURNED TO THE CITY. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

calendar.matters:
Respondents City And County Of San Francisco, Board Of Supervisors Of The City And County Of San Francisco, San Francisco Planning Commission And Real Parties In Interest A.F. Evans Development, Inc., Openhouse And The Regents Of The University Of California'S Hearing On Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Dec-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Petitioner Charlotte Daniels Writ Of Mandate.
APPEARANCES: Charlotte Daniels, 626-9636 petitioner & moving party in propria persona; Audrey Williams Pearson, Deputy City Atty 554-4621 for respondents & opposing parties; Steven L. Vettel, Esq. of Farella Braun + Martel LLP 954-4400 for Real Parties In Interest & opposing parties A.F. EVANS DEVELOPMENT, INC. AND OPENHOUSE.
RULING: Discussed and petitioner's request for any additional time was denied by the Court, no good cause to continue the matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXHAUST HER ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES, FAILS TO CITE TO THE ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD OR APPLICABLE LAW. THE PROJECT HAS MET ITS CEQA OBLIGATIONS, IS NOT SUBJECT TO THE NATIONAL HISTORIC PRESERVATION ACT AND THE COURT HAS FOUND THAT SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE SUPPORTS DEFENDANT'S FINDINGS. Order and judgment signed in open court. On the record, Court ordered the administrative record be returned to the City. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Respondents City And County Of San Francisco, Board Of Supervisors Of The City And County Of San Francisco, San Francisco Planning Commission And Real Parties In Interest A.F. Evans Development, Inc., Openhouse And The Regents Of The University Of California'S Hearing On Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Oct-23-2008 Continued To Dec-18-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
